behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: A mock embedding service can return any dimension; only a real embedding API call reveals whether the actual service produces the correct dimensionality (e.g., 1536-dim for OpenAI). Switching embedding services without catching dimensionality mismatch is a real failure mode. Cannot be caught by unit or integration tests using mock embeddings.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real embedding model with a test query string, verify the returned vector has the exact dimensionality required by the vector database index (e.g., 1536-dimensional for OpenAI embeddings, different for other models).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test directly calls external embedding API; mocking would hide dimensionality mismatches.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: Cannot be tested at unit level without mocking the vector database—mocking hides failures in how the similarity search is actually performed or results are ordered. Requires wiring together the vector DB client and search logic with known test vectors and measured similarity scores. A test DB with known data is sufficient; production infrastructure not needed.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a test vector database with known vectors at measured distances from a query point; perform a similarity search and verify that returned results are strictly ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs real vector DB coordination; mocks would pass even with broken ordering logic.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: Testing this requires a real LLM call—mocking the LLM would trivially pass or fail based on mock configuration, not the model's actual semantic understanding. The behavior is about the LLM's capability to recognize nuance and reorder results accordingly. Test result depends on real LLM output, making it agentic-level (not just a system-level API call). Captures the non-deterministic behavior of real language models.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a query with intentionally nuanced or ambiguous intent to the re-ranking step with real LLM backend, compare result ordering against the raw similarity-based ranking, and assert they differ in at least one position.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Mocking the LLM hides whether it actually performs semantic re-ranking; only real LLM output validates the behavior.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: Unit-level tests with mocked vector DB would hide failures in how the real DB's similarity threshold is applied or how empty results are handled. Requires integration of the similarity search step with a real (test) vector database populated with semantically unrelated data. Must verify end-to-end behavior: threshold is applied and empty set is returned, not default/low-relevance fallback.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a test vector database with documents semantically unrelated to a test query; perform a similarity search with an appropriate relevance threshold and verify the result set is empty rather than containing low-relevance items.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Mocks could be configured to return anything; need real vector DB to verify threshold behavior.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: Latency cannot be meaningfully tested with mocks or isolated components—mocks are artificially fast and miss bottlenecks in real infrastructure. Requires full pipeline execution with real embedding service, real vector database, and real LLM re-ranking in sequence. Only at workflow level can you measure true end-to-end latency across all components and detect regressions in any stage. Agentic-level tests (e.g., LLM only) miss latency issues in embedding or vector search.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the end-to-end search pipeline (embedding, vector search, LLM re-ranking) against realistic test data and actual infrastructure components; measure elapsed time from query input to final ranked results and assert median latency stays below the 2-second threshold.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Latency only measurable with real infrastructure wired end-to-end; component-level tests miss total time.
